Title: Expanding the Inositol Pyrophosphate Toolbox: Stereoselective Synthesis and Application of PP-InsP4 Isomers in Plant Signaling

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type (root extracts under phosphate starvation for metabolite assignment); recombinant protein used for in vitro assays
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 5-position phosphorylation/pyrophosphorylation branch of partially phosphorylated inositol phosphates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: [6-OH]-InsP5; [3-OH]-InsP5; also [4-OH]-InsP5 for indirect formation of 5-PP-InsP5 in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Selectively phosphorylates certain InsP5 isomers at the 5-position to generate corresponding 5-PP-InsP4 isomers; additionally can catalyze monophosphorylation of specific InsP5 isomers to InsP6 followed by pyrophosphorylation to 5-PP-InsP5 under defined in vitro conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PP-InsPs regulate phosphate homeostasis and energy metabolism in plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots under phosphate starvation for the unidentified PP-InsP4 species
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis ITPK1 selectively phosphorylates [6-OH]-InsP5 to [6-OH]-5-PP-InsP4 in vitro. 2. The study additionally shows phosphorylation of [3-OH]-InsP5 to [3-OH]-5-PP-InsP4, refining prior substrate-specificity assignments. 3. ITPK1 also generates 5-PP-InsP5 from [3-OH]- and [4-OH]-InsP5, suggesting sequential monophosphorylation to InsP6 followed by pyrophosphorylation. 4. None of the synthesized 5-PP-InsP4 isomers matches the phosphate-starvation-induced root PP-InsP4 peak, so ITPK1 is unlikely to generate that unknown in vivo species under phosphate starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays with HIS-MBP-tagged Arabidopsis ITPK1; CE-MS product detection using synthetic unlabeled and 18O-labeled PP-InsP4 standards; co-migration with 13C-labeled 5-PP-InsP5 standard; spiking of Arabidopsis root extracts; negative controls lacking ITPK1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes in vitro formation of specific 5-PP-InsP4 isomers and 5-PP-InsP5 from selected InsP5 substrates; not responsible for the unknown phosphate-starvation-induced root PP-InsP4 isomer detected in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation for Arabidopsis root metabolite profiling; defined in vitro incubation with ATP, creatine kinase, and phosphocreatine for enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Physiological relevance of the alternative IPK1-independent route to InsP6 and 5-PP-InsP5 formation remains unestablished because the activity was observed only under defined in vitro conditions; ITPK1 shows substrate promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: CE-MS metabolite profiling; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ITPK1 is a candidate enzyme to engineer inositol pyrophosphate composition and phosphate-signaling outputs, but deployment requires caution because substrate specificity is selective and the in vivo enzyme producing the starvation-induced root PP-InsP4 remains unidentified
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper distinguishes source species and applied species only for Arabidopsis ITPK1. Previous suggestions that the unknown root PP-InsP4 is a 5-PP-InsP4 isomer are rejected. The work also suggests a previously unrecognized IPK1-independent route to InsP6 biosynthesis for certain InsP5 isomers in vitro.
